title: zornia diphylla var. reticulata
intro: Zornia diphylla var. reticulata, also known as Reticulated Zornia or Wild Madder, is a flowering plant species in the family Leguminosae (Fabaceae). It is traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including relief from fever, pain, and skin irritations, as well as potential antimicrobial and antioxidant effects. Zornia diphylla var. reticulata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal use.
